answer:
\boxed{Davy Crockett}

Margaret Ezell’s essay *"The Myth of Davy Crockett"* (1978) critically examines the exaggerated, romanticized legend of the frontier icon, exposing how his story was mythologized to fit 19th-century American ideals. The work debunks the idea of Crockett as a lone, heroic figure, instead revealing his complex, flawed, and often contradictory legacy. This remains one of the most widely referenced critiques of frontier mythology in American history.
